name: Malosol'nye Ogurtsy - Brined Cucumbers Maloss
recipeIngredient: 1/4 lb sour cherry and/or black currant leaves
3.5 tbsp non-iodized salt per 2 quarts water
recipeInstructions:
1. Prepare the cucumbers following the instructions for Brined Cucumbers.
2. Rinse the dill and divide into two parts. Scrub and rinse the horseradish, then slice thinly. Peel and halve the garlic. Wash all leaves separately.
3. Place half of the dill in the bottom of a jar. Scatter half of the horseradish and garlic, along with one-third of the leaves on top.
4. Arrange half of the cucumbers upright, packing tightly. Cover with half of the remaining leaves. Fill with the rest of the cucumbers in the same manner.
5. Top with layers of the remaining horseradish, dill, garlic, and leaves.
6. Combine salt and water to create a brine (2.8-3.0% salt concentration). Stir until dissolved.
7. Pour the brine into the jar, ensuring full coverage. Place a saucer inside the jar with a weight (e.g., small rock).
8. Cover the mouth of the jar with two layers of cheesecloth and secure with string.
9. Store in a cool, airy room at 64-68°F for 3-4 days, removing scum as it forms and washing the jar's opening daily.
10. After 3-4 days, remove the weight and lid. Cover the jar with its own lid and refrigerate.
11. Malossol cucumbers are ready to eat in 1-2 days. Due to lower salt content than Brined Cucumbers, they should be consumed sooner.
12. Store pickles on the refrigerator's lowest shelf for up to 4-5 days before becoming too salty or mushy.
